title: Two Sum
description: Given an array of integers nums and an integer target, return indices of the two numbers such that they add up to target.
hints: Try using a hash map to store visited numbers.
Can you solve it in one pass?

title: Reverse Linked List
description: Given the head of a singly linked list, reverse the list, and return the reversed list.
constraints: The number of nodes in the list is in the range [0, 5000].
hints: Iterate through the list and reverse pointers one by one.

title: Valid Parentheses
description: Given a string s containing just the characters '(', ')', '{', '}', '[' and ']', determine if the input string is valid.
hints: Use a stack to keep track of opening brackets.
Check that each closing bracket matches the most recent opening bracket.

title: Merge Intervals
description: Given an array of intervals where intervals[i] = [start_i, end_i], merge all overlapping intervals, and return an array of the non-overlapping intervals that cover all the intervals in the input.
hints: Iterate through and merge overlapping ranges.

title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
description: Given a string s, find the length of the longest substring without repeating characters.

examples.explanation: The answer is 'abc', with the length of 3.

constraints: s consists of English letters, digits, symbols and spaces.
hints: Use a sliding window with a set to track unique characters.
Move the left pointer when a duplicate is found.

title: Word Ladder
description: Given two words, beginWord and endWord, and a dictionary wordList, return the length of the shortest transformation sequence from beginWord to endWord, such that only one letter can be changed at a time and each transformed word must exist in the word list.
constraints: All words are lowercase English letters.
hints: Think of words as nodes and transformations as edges.
Use BFS to find the shortest path.
